intro: Taenianthera dammeri, also known as Dammera, is a small, shrub-like plant native to tropical Australia. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Dammera is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Dammera include dammarane-type triterpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Dammera is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
